summary: New design doc claim_stack_design_2026-05-11.md (8 sections ~600 lines). Proposes claim_v1 schema alongside the 6 existing substrate_block schemas — additive, no kernel contract change. Each claim carries natural-language statement + expected-verifier label + expected-verdict label + trust tier. Runner module tier_1_claim_runner.py loads batch, dispatches to 7 verifier fns (citation_audit, catalog_lookup, mpmath_compute, sympy_factor, triangulation, substrate_self_check, manual_review), emits kernel CLAIM/FALSIFY/PROMOTE per claim, wraps result in existing LearnerRecord enrichment with 3 claim-specific extension fields. Moves central opcode from shipped-but-unused to exercised-at-every-claim. THREE CATEGORIES Aporia uniquely positioned to author: (1) frontier_survey claims from anti_anchor/catalog_edit blocks the substrate-shaped pipeline produces (each anti_anchor → paired false_form/true_form couplet); (2) calibration claims from training_anchor blocks (each labeled instance → one verifiable claim); (3) boundary claims from tensor catalog T#NN entries (each bound interval → 4-6 claims spanning within-range/falsification-probe/open-gap/exact-value-unknown). Fourth lower-volume category substrate_self Techne authors. QUALITY DISCIPLINE rules A/B/C enforced at runner load: per-batch category diversity ≥3/4 categories; expected-verdict mix ~40% survived / 25% falsified / 25% open / 10% conditional; trust-tier balance ml_predicted ≤10%, analytical+numerical ≥70%. Rule D suggested-not-enforced: each batch include ≥1 claim author SUSPECTS will be wrong. Worked examples in doc: paired AA-001 GCT_OCCURRENCE_DEAD frontier_survey couplet; KnotInfo Alexander-poly trefoil calibration; 4 boundary claims for T#4 M⟨3⟩ rank; 2 substrate_self meta-claims testing TriangulationProtocol. 8 OPEN QUESTIONS for Aporia: volume per batch (~50 start), calibration sampling vs hand-pick, kernel three-verdict + claim open (caveat metadata recommended over new field), composition_rule verification path (literature_audit pilot, defer Triangulation to Tier-2), author split (Techne substrate_self / Aporia other 3), persistence model (in-memory Tier-1 default), failure budget (timeouts → inconclusive; flag batch if >20% inconclusive), pilot ordering (recommend parallel with substrate-shaped pipeline). Honest constraints: random claims = volume noise; quality discipline is the part that matters. ml_predicted cap 10%. No kernel contract change. All within file ownership. Pilot scope ~5-7 days joint work for first 50-claim batch + runner execution + iteration cycle.
behavior_delta: exercises Sigma-kernel CLAIM/FALSIFY/PROMOTE opcodes at every claim — substrate moves from shipped-but-unused to load-bearing per HARD WARNING remediation
